quiz_items.question: Which of the following are required responsibilities of laser product manufacturers under FDA regulations? (Select all that apply)
options: Design and manufacture products to comply with the standard
Submit Annual Reports to CDRH by September 1 each year
Conduct quarterly safety inspections at customer sites
quiz_items.explanation: Manufacturers must: design/manufacture for compliance, test products, certify compliance, maintain records, submit reports (including Annual Reports), report accidental radiation occurrences, report defects/noncompliances, and conduct recalls when necessary. Quarterly customer site inspections are not a specific regulatory requirement.

quiz_items.question: OEM laser products (components sold to other manufacturers) are completely exempt from all FDA reporting requirements.
quiz_items.explanation: False. While OEM products have reduced requirements, manufacturers must still register and submit minimal information including product name, model number, and laser medium or wavelength per 21 CFR 1040.10(a)(3).

quiz_items.question: A U.S. company manufactures laser products exclusively for export to Europe and labels them 'For Export Only - Not for Use in USA.' The products comply with EU regulations but not 21 CFR 1040.10. Is this compliant?
options: No, all lasers manufactured in the U.S. must comply with 21 CFR 1040.10
Yes, if labeled as export-only and complying with importing country requirements
Only if the company also obtains a variance from CDRH
Only if the lasers are Class I or II
quiz_items.explanation: Products intended only for export, properly labeled as such, and complying with the importing country's requirements are exempt from the standard per 21 CFR 1010.20.
